mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: The Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that continuously scans your cluster for security issues, generating detailed reports on vulnerabilities, configuration audits, and compliance. It is ideal for organizations looking to enhance their security posture in Kubernetes environments. Key trade-offs include the need for resource allocation for scanning and potential performance impacts during heavy workloads.

steps.title: Add the Trivy Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Trivy Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add aquasecurity https://aquasecurity.github.io/trivy-operator

```

> This repository contains the Helm charts for the Trivy Operator.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Trivy Operator in the 'trivy-system' namespace, ensuring that the necessary Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) are also installed.

```bash

helm install trivy-operator aquasecurity/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --create-namespace --version 0.32.1

```

> This installs the Trivy Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Trivy Operator pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Look for the 'trivy-operator' pod to be in 'Running' status.

steps.title: Create a sample Custom Resource for scanning
steps.description:
This step creates a sample TrivyReport Custom Resource to initiate a scan on a specified workload.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: TrivyReport
metadata:
  name: example-scan
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: your-deployment-name
EOF

```

> Replace 'your-deployment-name' with the actual name of your deployment.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: TrivyReport
metadata:
  name: example-scan
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: your-deployment-name
EOF

steps.title: Check the scan results
steps.description:
This command retrieves the scan results generated by the Trivy Operator.

```bash

kubectl get trivyreport -n default

```

> Look for the 'example-scan' report to see the results of the scan.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that any generated reports and configurations will be lost.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator -n trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> This command uninstalls the Trivy Operator from the specified namespace.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important configurations. This process will upgrade the Trivy Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get trivyreport -n default -o yaml > trivyreport-backup.yaml

helm upgrade trivy-operator aquasecurity/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.32.1

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Backup existing TrivyReports.
> This command upgrades the Trivy Operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get trivyreport -n default -o yaml > trivyreport-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Trivy Operator pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration or insufficient resources allocated to the pod.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n trivy-system' for errors and adjust resource limits if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: TrivyReport shows 'Pending' status
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Trivy Operator is unable to access the workload specified in the report.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the workload exists and is running. Use 'kubectl get deployments -n default' to verify.

troubleshooting.title: Helm install fails with 'Chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Helm repository may not be added correctly or is outdated.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm repo update' to refresh the repository and try the install command again.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl get pods returns no pods in 'trivy-system'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate that the Trivy Operator was not installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the Trivy Operator using the Helm install command and check for errors.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new compliance reports including CIS Kubernetes Benchmark v1.23 and enhanced RBAC scanning capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required when upgrading from earlier versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pod running in the 'trivy-system' namespace and the TrivyReport resources reflecting the scan results with a 'Ready' status.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: TrivyReport
metadata:
  name: example-scan
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: your-deployment-name
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
